subtasks.description: Generate SQL query to identify top 10 TPC-H customers by lifetime value (total order amount) filtered to market segment 'BUILDING', including customer ID, name, and aggregated metrics (total spend, order count, avg order value, date range)

Generate SQL query to extract detailed order pattern history for the top 10 customers identified above, including order dates, amounts, item counts, frequency intervals, and temporal trends to support narrative generation

Reconcile customer LTV metrics with order pattern data from both queries; classify each customer's engagement level and risk profile; generate personalized retention narrative for each of the 10 customers referencing their specific order patterns, spending trends, frequency changes, and relationship maturity

plan.cost_rationale: 3 subtasks minimum: (1) Initial SQL query to rank top 10 BUILDING segment customers by LTV requires moderate complexity (aggregation with segment filtering); (2) Follow-up SQL to extract detailed order patterns for those 10 customers is needed to support narrative generation and depends on first query results; (3) Cross-recon subtask synthesizes both datasets, classifies engagement/risk, and generates personalized narratives—frontier tier justified because it requires multi-signal analysis (LTV + temporal patterns + trend inference) to produce coherent, contextual retention messaging for 10 distinct customer profiles. Cross-recon handles classification and narrative generation as integrated output, avoiding separate downstream agents.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value, MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date, MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10

WITH top_customers AS (SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count, AVG(o.o_totalprice) AS avg_order_value, MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date, MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date FROM customer c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10) SELECT tc.c_custkey, tc.c_name, tc.total_spend, tc.order_count, tc.avg_order_value, tc.first_order_date, tc.last_order_date, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, o.o_orderstatus, o.o_orderpriority, COUNT(l.l_linenumber) AS item_count, SUM(l.l_quantity) AS total_quantity, LAG(o.o_orderdate) OVER (PARTITION BY o.o_custkey ORDER BY o.o_orderdate) AS prev_order_date, (o.o_orderdate - LAG(o.o_orderdate) OVER (PARTITION BY o.o_custkey ORDER BY o.o_orderdate)) AS days_since_prev_order FROM top_customers tc INNER JOIN orders o ON tc.c_custkey = o.o_custkey LEFT JOIN lineitem l ON o.o_orderkey = l.l_orderkey GROUP BY tc.c_custkey, tc.c_name, tc.total_spend, tc.order_count, tc.avg_order_value, tc.first_order_date, tc.last_order_date, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, o.o_orderstatus, o.o_orderpriority ORDER BY tc.c_custkey, o.o_orderdate
